meta.name: DashCommerce starter
meta.description: A Stripe-powered storefront with 6 demo products spanning every DashCommerce product type.
meta.author: DashCommerce

settings.title: My Shop
settings.tagline: A Stripe-powered storefront, editable entirely from the admin.

fields.label: Featured Image
fields.help: Primary image used on the PDP and in shop listings.

fields.label: SKU
fields.help: Stock-keeping unit. Must be unique across all products.

fields.label: Prices
fields.help: Set a price in each currency your store accepts. Customers whose cart is set to an unpriced currency will not be able to add this product.

fields.label: Low-stock Threshold
fields.help: Triggers the LowStockAlerts widget when stock crosses below this value.

fields.label: Weight (g)
fields.help: Used by weight-based shipping calculations.

fields.label: Shipping Class
fields.help: Must match a ShippingClass slug configured in DashCommerce → Shipping.

fields.label: Downloadable Files
fields.help: JSON array. Each entry: { "name": "Manual.pdf", "mediaId": "01K..." } or { "name": "...", "url": "https://..." }. Only used when Downloadable is on.

fields.label: Subscription Config
fields.help: Required for subscription products. JSON: { "interval": "month", "intervalCount": 1, "trialDays": 7 }. Interval is one of day | week | month | year.

fields.label: Child Products (for grouped type)
fields.help: JSON array of product ULIDs, e.g. ["01KP...", "01KP..."]. Only used when Product Type = grouped.

fields.label: Product URL (for external type)
fields.help: Affiliate / external link. Only used when Product Type = external.

fields.label: Vendor
fields.help: Marketplace vendor whose Stripe Connect account receives funds for this product. Only active when multi-vendor is enabled in Settings.

collections.label: Hero Slides
collections.description: Rotating banner shown at the top of the home page.

fields.label: Eyebrow
fields.help: Small label above the headline (e.g. 'New arrivals').

fields.label: Headline
fields.help: Main display text. Use line breaks for visual line wrapping.

fields.label: Subtitle
fields.help: Short supporting copy under the headline.

fields.label: CTA link
fields.help: Relative or absolute URL the primary button navigates to.

fields.label: Background type
fields.help: Switching this reveals only the fields you need. The storefront also auto-picks Video URL > Image > Gradient as a fallback, so uploading a richer medium 'just works' even if this stays on a simpler mode.

fields.label: Gradient / CSS (fallback background)
fields.help: Used when no image or video is set. Any valid CSS background — paste a gradient, solid colour, or image() function. Also shows behind a video while it loads.

fields.label: Background image
fields.help: If set, takes precedence over the gradient. Recommended: 2400×1200, JPG/WebP. Leave empty to use the gradient.

fields.label: Image focal point
fields.help: Applies when a background image is set. CSS object-position (e.g. 'center', 'top right', '30% 60%').

fields.label: Video URL (highest priority)
fields.help: If set, overrides both the image and the gradient. Paste a direct MP4/WebM URL, a YouTube link, or a Vimeo link. Leave empty to use the image or gradient instead.

fields.label: Video poster (fallback image)
fields.help: Shown while the video loads or on devices that block autoplay.

fields.label: Overlay (over image/video)
fields.help: CSS background layered over image/video backgrounds to keep the text legible. Use e.g. 'rgba(0,0,0,.45)' for a flat dim, or a gradient for directional shading.

fields.label: Accent bar
fields.help: CSS background for the thin bar at the bottom of the slide.

fields.label: Sort order
fields.help: Slides display in ascending order. Lower numbers first.

collections.label: Posts
collections.description: Editorial content — product stories, buying guides, and behind-the-scenes. Shown on the homepage 'From the journal' section and the /blog index.

fields.label: Excerpt
fields.help: Short summary shown on listing cards and meta descriptions. 140–180 chars.

fields.label: Cover image
fields.help: Hero image at the top of the article and the thumbnail on listing cards.

fields.label: Cover fallback gradient
fields.help: Shown when no cover image is set. Any valid CSS background.

fields.label: Author name
fields.help: Display byline. Leave blank to hide the author line.

fields.label: Published date
fields.help: Override the auto publish time if you want a specific display date.

fields.label: Reading time (minutes)
fields.help: Estimated reading time shown next to the author line.

fields.label: Body
fields.help: Rich article content — supports headings, paragraphs, lists, quotes, images, and embeds.

collections.label: Banners
collections.description: Reusable promo blocks. Look them up by slug from any page template (e.g. the homepage 'Our promise' section is the `home-promise` banner).

fields.label: Internal name
fields.help: Admin-only label. Not shown on the storefront.

fields.label: Eyebrow
fields.help: Short uppercase kicker above the headline (e.g. 'Our promise').

fields.label: Headline
fields.help: Main banner title. Use `\n` to force a line break.

fields.label: Body
fields.help: Rich body copy — supports bold, links, and short lists.

fields.label: Secondary CTA label
fields.help: Optional second CTA. Rendered as an outline button next to the primary.

fields.label: Background type
fields.help: Switching this reveals only the fields you need. The storefront also auto-picks Video URL > Image > Gradient as a fallback.

fields.label: Gradient / CSS (fallback background)
fields.help: Used when no image or video is set. Any valid CSS background.

fields.label: Background image
fields.help: If set, takes precedence over the gradient. Leave empty to use the gradient.

fields.label: Image focal point
fields.help: Applies when a background image is set. CSS object-position (e.g. 'center', 'top right', '30% 60%').

fields.label: Video URL (highest priority)
fields.help: If set, overrides both the image and the gradient. Paste a direct MP4/WebM URL, a YouTube link, or a Vimeo link.

fields.label: Overlay (over image/video)
fields.help: CSS background layered over image/video for legibility. E.g. 'rgba(0,0,0,.45)'.

collections.label: Trust Items
collections.description: Homepage 'trust strip' items — small benefit-bullets shown above the footer (free shipping, secure checkout, etc.).

fields.label: Icon / symbol
fields.help: A single-character symbol or emoji used as the item's visual mark.

fields.label: Sort order
fields.help: Lower numbers appear first. Leave gaps (10, 20, 30) to make reordering easy.

collections.label: Category Tiles
collections.description: Powers the homepage 'Browse by type' bento grid. Each tile links to a category page and has a size that controls how it lays out.

fields.label: Label
fields.help: Big title shown on the tile (e.g. 'Physical Goods').

fields.label: Description
fields.help: Small kicker shown above the label.

fields.label: Link
fields.help: Where the tile goes when clicked (e.g. '/category/physical-goods').

fields.label: Size
fields.help: Controls how the tile lays out in the bento grid.

fields.label: Background gradient / CSS
fields.help: Any valid CSS background. Used as a fallback behind the image.

fields.label: Background image
fields.help: Optional. If set, shown over the gradient with a dark overlay.

collections.label: Announcements
collections.description: Messages shown in the top bar and/or the homepage marquee strip. Toggle `enabled` to show/hide without deleting.

fields.label: Link (optional)
fields.help: If set, the message becomes a link.

collections.label: Site Text
collections.description: Footer-specific copy that doesn't belong on native Site Settings — newsletter strip, copyright template, and overrides for the footer tagline. Identity (title, tagline, logo, social) lives under Site Settings. Homepage section headers live under Section Headers.

fields.label: Footer tagline override
fields.help: Leave blank to use the site tagline from Site Settings. Set to customise the footer brand block specifically.

fields.label: Footer newsletter body
fields.defaultValue: New arrivals, exclusive offers, and carefully chosen recommendations.

fields.label: Footer copyright template
fields.help: Placeholders: `{year}` is the current year, `{title}` is the site title from Site Settings.

fields.label: Social · Pinterest URL
fields.help: Instagram/Twitter and other major channels are managed on Site Settings. Pinterest lives here because EmDash's native social schema doesn't include it yet.

collections.label: Section Headers
collections.description: Eyebrow + title + optional body & CTA for each content band (homepage Bento, Products, Journal, etc.). Each entry can optionally carry a full background (gradient / image / video) so section intros can coordinate with the visuals of the items beneath them.

fields.label: Admin label
fields.help: Internal name shown in the admin list — e.g. 'Homepage · Bento'. Not rendered on the site.

fields.label: Eyebrow
fields.help: Small uppercase kicker shown above the title (e.g. 'Browse by type').

fields.label: Title
fields.help: Main heading. Use `\n` to force a line break — the second line is rendered italic to match the homepage style.

fields.label: Subtitle
fields.help: Optional supporting paragraph under the title.

fields.label: Render style
fields.help: Compact mirrors the plain section intros of most e-commerce sites. Band wraps the header in a full-width panel using the background fields below — use this when the items below have their own strong visuals (e.g. the Bento tiles).

fields.label: Background type
fields.help: Only applies when render style is 'Band'. Switching reveals only the fields you need.

fields.label: Image focal point
fields.help: CSS object-position (e.g. 'center', 'top right', '30% 60%').

fields.label: Video URL
fields.help: MP4/WebM direct URL, YouTube, or Vimeo link.

fields.label: Overlay (over image/video)
fields.help: CSS layer placed over image/video backgrounds to keep the text legible.

terms.label: Physical Goods
terms.description: Tangible products that ship — apparel, ceramics, accessories, and everyday objects.

terms.label: Digital Downloads
terms.description: Instantly-delivered files — templates, presets, fonts, e-books, or any downloadable asset.

terms.label: Subscriptions
terms.description: Recurring products — monthly boxes, memberships, and any product billed on a schedule.

terms.label: Bundles & Sets
terms.description: Grouped products sold together as a set, typically at a discount.

terms.label: Bestseller
terms.description: Our most-loved products of the season.

terms.label: Limited Edition
terms.description: Small-run drops. Once they're gone, they're gone.

terms.label: Eco-Friendly
terms.description: Made from recycled, renewable, or low-impact materials.

terms.label: Great as a Gift
terms.description: Thoughtful picks that make good presents.

terms.label: Behind the Scenes
terms.description: How products are made, sourced, or designed. Craft, process, and provenance stories.

terms.label: Product Stories
terms.description: Deep dives on individual products — why they're in the edit, what makes them worth owning.

terms.label: Buying Guides
terms.description: Practical, gift-style roundups that help readers pick the right product for the job.

items.label: Journal
items.titleAttr: Product stories, buying guides and behind-the-scenes

data.headline:
Curated Monthly,
For You
data.subtitle: A thoughtfully assembled box of goods, delivered to your door every month.

data.title: The Craft of Enamel: Behind Our Signature Mugs
data.excerpt: Every enamel mug in The Edit is dipped and fired by hand. Here's what goes into one.

children.text: Enamelware sits at a strange intersection of the industrial and the handmade. At a glance, our signature mug looks like a mass-produced kitchen staple. Up close — once you know what to look for — every piece is unmistakably the work of a small team in a single workshop.
From blank steel to finished piece

Each mug begins life as a pressed steel blank. The blanks are pickled to remove any mill scale, then dipped twice in a liquid enamel slurry. Between dips, they're hung on a rack to dry for at least four hours — rush it, and you get hairline cracks that show up only after firing.
Pressed steel blanks sourced from a Midlands fabricator
Two-coat dipping (base + topcoat) to resist chipping
Fired at 820°C for 180 seconds, then air-cooled
Inspected for pinholes, glossed rims, and weight parity
Why we fire at 820°C

Most factory enamelware is fired hotter and faster to move product. At 820°C the coat fuses a touch more gently — it takes longer per piece, but the resulting surface is harder and the colour sits a little deeper.
The difference isn't visible from across the room. It's visible on the lip of the mug, in your hand, every morning for ten years.
We've had customers send us photos of our mugs still going strong after a decade of daily use. That's the point of enamel — it should outlast the kitchen you bought it for.

data.title: Why We Ship Monthly Boxes Instead of Bigger Catalogues
data.excerpt: Three years in, subscription boxes are still our favourite product. Here's why constraint beats abundance.

children.text: When we launched the Monthly Box, the internal pitch was half-serious: 'make the smallest possible product with the largest possible meaning.' Three years and 14,000 boxes later, it's still our highest-NPS line.
Constraint is the product

A monthly box forces us to pick four things we genuinely believe in. Not forty. Not four hundred. That's the feature — it filters out the noise on behalf of the customer, because we've done the filtering for them.
Our customers don't subscribe for the 12% discount. They subscribe because 'four good things a month' is a better editorial contract than 'an unlimited online shop.'
How we plan a year ahead
February each year, we lock the theme for the next twelve months.
Each month gets a lead item (~50% of the box value) and three supporting items.
We try to ensure one digital good per quarter — it keeps the ratio balanced.
Final composition signs off ten weeks before the ship date.
The best subscription products feel like letters from a friend with better taste than you. Ours tries to read that way.

data.title: In Defence of the Logo Tee
data.excerpt: The plain T-shirt is having a moment again. We make the case for putting a small mark back on it.

children.text: Fashion has spent the last decade convinced the future of the T-shirt is quiet. Heavyweight cotton. No branding. A small tonal label that you had to squint to read. We loved it too — for a while.
The case for a small mark

A logo, done sparingly, does two things a blank tee can't. It tells the wearer this piece is part of something — a shop, a scene, a point of view. And it lets other people recognise it without a caption.
How we approached ours
2cm wide, centred at the chest — smaller than a coin.
Tonal embroidery, not print: matte thread on matte fabric.
220gsm ringspun cotton, pre-washed. Same body as the blank.
The goal isn't to make the logo the point. The goal is to make the logo the reason the tee has a point.

data.title: How to Pick the Right Subscription Box
data.excerpt: Four questions we ask any customer who's on the fence between our Monthly, Quarterly, and Annual plans.

children.text: Subscription fatigue is real. Before you sign up for anything — ours or anyone else's — ask yourself these four things.
1. How often do you actually need new things?
If the honest answer is 'rarely,' a monthly box will sit unopened and you'll resent it. Quarterly or annual is almost always the better pick.
2. Do you trust the curator?
Subscription boxes live or die on taste. Read one issue of the curator's newsletter or look at their Instagram. If their eye doesn't match yours, a discount won't fix it.
3. Can you pause?
Most good subscriptions let you skip or pause. Check this before you sign up — not after.
4. What's the exit?
Look for a one-click cancel link inside the account page. If you can't find it in the T&Cs, that's your answer.

data.title: The Quiet Return of the Digital Download
data.excerpt: Streaming was meant to be the end of ownership. Our digital download numbers have tripled. Here's what's going on.

children.text: A quiet thing has been happening inside the catalogue. Over the last 18 months, our digital download sales have grown three times faster than the rest of the shop. They're a quarter of revenue now.
People are buying again
A decade of 'streaming-everything' built a generation of customers who realised they don't own any of the things they pay for. A paid-once, forever-downloadable file is, by contrast, radically calm.
What's selling
Lightroom + Capture One presets — up 312% YoY.
High-res icon packs — up 180%.
Brush and texture sets — up 145%.
Printable zines — surprisingly strong, up 98%.
Digital goods are having a second wind because owning something — anything — feels novel again.

data.title: The Gifting Guide for People Who Don't Do Gifts
data.excerpt: Seven quietly excellent things from The Edit, picked for the hardest person on your list: the one who says they don't want anything.

children.text: Every year someone on your list says they don't want anything. They're usually lying — they just don't want bad things. Here's what we'd pick.
Under £30
The enamel mug. Nothing about it screams gift, which is exactly why it works as one. A mug that looks good on a shelf for ten years is a better present than a scented candle they'll burn in three.
Under £75
Pair the logo tee with the starter bundle — it lands as a thoughtful set rather than one obvious thing.
Under £150
The quarterly subscription. It spreads the gift across four seasons; you get to be the 'something nice showed up today' friend three more times.

The secret to gifting people who don't do gifts is to give them permission to keep the thing around without feeling guilty about it. Quietly excellent objects — mugs, tees, well-designed software — pass that test.
Every product in The Edit is selected for quality, function, and lasting value. We don't chase trends — we curate things worth keeping.
